frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: neural encoding should match the 1/f^2 statistics of natural temporal variation

frameworks.name: post-receptoral channels
frameworks.one_line: achromatic and chromatic pathways with distinct temporal sensitivity functions

core_question: Does behavioral encoding fidelity for flicker frequency differ by post-receptoral direction or photopic light level?
key_assumptions: 1/f^2 temporal statistics are shared across achromatic and chromatic modulations
efficient coding predicts encoding fidelity follows input statistics, not neural tuning
memory-based frequency matching reads out perceptual encoding
manipulations: post-receptoral direction (luminance LMS vs L-M chromatic)
key_findings: encoding fidelity across frequency was similar for achromatic and chromatic modulations
encoding fidelity did not differ between high and low photopic light levels
interpretations: behavioral fidelity reflects shared input statistics rather than differences in neural temporal tuning
consistent with efficient-coding prediction that encoding follows the 1/f^2 environmental prior
